title: calotis hispidula
intro: Calotis hispidula, also known as the hairy burr or prickly daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Australia, for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and infections. Herbal preparations of Calotis hispidula include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, often used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
